summary: Iteration 133: Mark As Paid Status Change Tests - ALL 11 TESTS PASS. Backend endpoint PATCH /api/invoices/{id}/status with {status: 'pagata'} correctly updates: status='pagata', payment_status='pagata', totale_pagato=total_document, residuo=0. Valid transitions (emessa/inviata_sdi/accettata/scaduta → pagata) work correctly. Invalid transitions (bozza→pagata, pagata→pagata) correctly rejected with 400.

invalid_status_transitions.bozza_to_pagata: PASS - Correctly rejected with 400 'Transizione non valida: bozza -> pagata'
invalid_status_transitions.pagata_to_pagata: PASS - Correctly rejected with 400 'Transizione non valida: pagata -> pagata'

payment_fields_calculation.totale_pagato: PASS - Set to totals.total_document when marking as paid
payment_fields_calculation.residuo: PASS - Set to 0 when marking as paid
payment_fields_calculation.payment_status: PASS - Set to 'pagata' when marking as paid

get_invoices_response.includes_payment_fields: PASS - GET /api/invoices/ returns totale_pagato, residuo, payment_status

critical_code_review_comments: Backend code at /app/backend/routes/invoices.py lines 579-584 correctly handles 'pagata' status transition
update_fields correctly sets payment_status, totale_pagato, and residuo when status='pagata'
Valid transitions defined at lines 557-566 correctly include paths to 'pagata' from emessa, inviata_sdi, accettata, scaduta
Invalid transitions from bozza and pagata correctly rejected with 400 error
test_credentials: Test user/session created via mongosh setup fixture
seed_data_creation: 6 test invoices created with different statuses (emessa, inviata_sdi, accettata, scaduta, bozza, pagata)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mark As Paid backend functionality verified working correctly. The PATCH /api/invoices/{id}/status endpoint properly handles 'pagata' status transitions and updates payment fields (payment_status, totale_pagato, residuo). Frontend testing skipped per instruction (Google OAuth required). Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration133_mark_as_paid.py can be reused for regression testing.
